---
title: "Pine Barren Beaksedge (Rhynchospora pineticola)"
slug: "rhynchospora-pineticola"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/249167696/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pine Barren Beaksedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/249167696/medium.jpeg" alt="Pine Barren Beaksedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/stevecollins">
stevecollins</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/145089672">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167892)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare species in Florida found in moist to wet, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its slender stems and clusters of spikelets with distinctive bristles. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet, sandy habitats such as pine flatwoods, savannas, and seepage areas. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to plant diversity and structure in fire-maintained wetland ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167892-Rhynchospora-pineticola)
-   [Local profile](https://fsus.ncbg.unc.edu/main.php?pg=show-taxon-detail.php&taxonid=6826)
